title: oxalis copiosa
intro: Oxalis corymbosa, also known as the wood sorrel, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Oxalidaceae family. It has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and stems, which contain oxalic acid and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The wood sorrel has been used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and nausea, due to its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as its ability to balance the pH levels in the body. Herbal preparations of Oxalis corymbosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and provide antioxidant benefits.
